Tests a regression issue related to the behavior of Array and RegExp prototypes.

# Purpose
This code demonstrates a sequence of operations on JavaScript prototypes and error handling. It first adds an element to the `Array` prototype using the `push` method, which is unconventional as it modifies the prototype of a built-in object. It then freezes the `Array` prototype with `Object.freeze`, preventing further modifications to it. The code attempts to execute the `exec` method on the `RegExp` constructor's prototype, wrapped in a `try-catch` block to handle any potential errors that may occur during execution. This script is likely used to test or demonstrate the behavior of JavaScript's prototype manipulation and error handling mechanisms.
